title: inocarpus glabellus
intro: Inocarpus glabellus, also known as the Tamanu tree, is a plant species native to the Pacific Island region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its rich oil extracted from its seeds, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Inocarpus glabellus include wound healing, skin regeneration, and anti-aging effects, due to its rich antioxidants and fatty acid composition. Traditionally, the oil is extracted and used in herbal preparations, such as topical creams and ointments, as well as in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and well-being.
